name: CYPHER DUNGEON
short_description: Identifier = ZERO. Localization = unknown system. Objectives = survive, decrypt, escape. Codes are key. Death is protocol. The sequence begins now.
detailed_description:
A MetroidBrainia About Decryption

Progress by learning, observing, and decoding.

  • Discover 78 hidden codes scattered throughout the dungeon

  • Only 13 codes stand between you and the ending (if you can find them)

  • Solve environmental puzzles where everything may hide a cypher

  • Unlock new paths through knowledge, not upgrades

Explore a Connected Cyber Dungeon

  • 49 interconnected rooms forming a single cohesive maze

  • Hidden shortcuts and secret paths

  • Deadly traps and mysterious mechanisms

  • Multiple ways to progress, depending on what you understand

Survive the System

  • Face 6 types of enemies guarding the dungeon

  • Discover 3 hidden powers that change how you explore

  • Choose between 3 difficulty modes adapted to your playstyle

Multiple Outcomes

Your understanding shapes your fate.

  • Approximately 4 hours to reach an ending

  • 3 different endings to uncover

Can you decrypt the dungeon and escape its system?

Awaken inside a sealed network, a dungeon built from silent data and algorithms. Fragments of information pulse beneath its surface, waiting to be uncovered and understood.

Each corner hides patterns, and each encounter a trace of logic. Progress is measured not by steps, but by comprehension. Failure is routine; reconstruction is protocol.

Dialogue and voice are absent; only the hum of corrupted circuits. Your path is defined by observation and decryption. Some discoveries open new routes, others deepen the mystery.

Beneath its minimal neon aesthetic, CYPHER DUNGEON offers a subtle blend of exploration and numerological puzzles in a dynamic adventure game.

Every symbol, every signal may hold meaning. The game has already begun.

supported_languages:
English*, French*, Italian*, German*, Spanish - Spain*, Simplified Chinese*, Korean*, Japanese*, Polish*, Portuguese - Brazil*, Russian*
*languages with full audio support

reviews.review_text: If you like puzzles and figuring stuff out, go for it. It's tough, but the "aha" moments feel good !

Disclaimer : I know the (solo) dev.

Small and to-the-point puzzle adventure game. While the top-down view and control scheme remind of 2D Zelda games for instance, it's a fairly different beast - the star of the show being the codes to find, rather than exploring and collecting stuff.

At the moment I can't figure out anything except the most basic, in-your-face ones, but that's on me for playing mostly games where my brain doesn't need to connect neurons. If you like the puzzly parts of games like Fez, Tunic or Animal Well, this is probably a nice bit of candy to feast upon.
